issues.summary: I read all 17 .md docs (README + 01–16). The doc set has a clear, well-banner'd "spine" — README + doc 12 establish the CURRENT terminal-first hybrid (libghostty + NetBird + read-only inspector), and most early docs (01, 03, 05, 07, 08, 09, 10, 11) carry a "GUI-path-only / superseded" banner at the top. That banner discipline is good. However the polish gaps cluster in two patterns: (1) FINAL decisions that were settled in the later docs (libghostty over SwiftTerm; TERM=xterm-ghostty; local-echo dropped; QUIC dropped; remote-over-mesh; 8-bit quality-mode codec) are STILL stated with the old value in places that were only banner-patched, not body-reconciled — most damagingly INSIDE the canonical doc 12 itself (TERM, local-echo) and in the cross-reference docs 01/08/09/13. (2) A few summary lines (README, doc 13) restate a decision with wrong/old wording (SwiftTerm; "CoT redacted"; "không predictive local-echo"). The single highest-severity finding is an internal contradiction within doc 12: §3 mandates porting the mosh PredictionEngine with DisplayPreference=Always as "the biggest responsiveness lever," while the same doc's technique-ranking (#11), roadmap (Phase 5), and doc 13 say local echo is NOT needed and is dropped. The second is doc 08 §4 still listing "remote/Internet/NAT = non-goal (LAN only)" which flatly contradicts the NetBird-mesh architecture. TERM value (xterm-256color vs the FINAL xterm-ghostty) drifts across 5 spots in doc 12 vs doc 14/README.

Đòn bẩy biên') and Phase 5 roadmap (line ~808 '~~Speculative local echo~~ — KHÔNG cần')
issues.problem: Doc 12 contradicts itself on the local-echo / PredictionEngine decision. But the same doc's technique ranking (#11) and the revised roadmap (Phase 5, struck through) declare predictive echo NOT needed and dropped, because NetBird P2P is assumed (echo benefit only matters on relayed/WAN). A reader cannot tell whether local echo is in or out.
issues.recommended_fix: Pick the FINAL decision (per doc 13 §4 + doc 12 roadmap Phase 5: local echo dropped for v1 because P2P is assumed) and make §3 + the summary table consistent with it. issues.summary: The doc set drifted because each research round was appended as a new self-contained doc (12, 13, 14, 15, 16) that re-explains decisions already covered elsewhere instead of pointing to a single source of truth. The single biggest redundancy problem is INSIDE doc 12 itself: it bundles five separate research outputs (hybrid arch, terminal streaming, GUI video, claude-code roadmap notes, NetBird-era roadmap) into one 811-line file that re-states the same corrections (forkpty/openpty, posix_openpt, IUTF8, sandbox, ttyd framing, mosh local-echo, 4:2:0/4:4:4, idle-skip) two or three times each, and still contains two blocks of orphaned authoring meta-commentary (lines 251-263, 660-662). Cross-doc, the same decisions are duplicated and drifting: (a) "drop app crypto / drop QUIC / no serviceClass" lives verbatim in 03, 13, and 12; (b) the local-echo/prediction decision is self-contradictory within 12 and between 12 and 13; (c) the "control plane needed despite P2P" + "duplicate-prompt dedup" + Claude auth/OAuth + SDK-skills-parity decisions each appear in 2-3 docs; (d) doc 13 declares itself the corrections hub but the actual corrections are scattered as inline banners across 01/02/03/04/05/07/08/09/10. Recommend: (1) split doc 12 into 3-4 focused docs or aggressively de-duplicate its summary-vs-detail sections; (2) designate explicit single sources of truth (13=transport/security, 12=arch+roadmap, 14=claude-code, 16=inspector) and replace re-explanations with pointers; (3) consolidate the scattered correction banners; (4) fix the stale QUIC recommendation in 08/01 and the prediction contradiction in 12. Genuine duplication only is flagged — I did not recommend merging docs that legitimately serve different readers.

issues.summary: Reviewed all 17 .md docs (README + 01–16). The biggest reader-facing problems are (1) the "PTF" typo for "PTY", which appears in 5 files (12, 14, 15, 16 confirmed by grep; total 9 occurrences) and is especially damaging because it lands in the CURRENT-architecture docs and in load-bearing decision text; (2) a stale terminal-renderer terminology split — README + docs 12/14/16 say the chosen client renderer is libghostty (SwiftTerm = fallback only), but 01-architecture.md §banner and 13-netbird-transport.md §4 still state SwiftTerm as THE renderer, and doc 12's entire terminal-design body (§1–6, lines 267–502) is still written SwiftTerm-first with SwiftTerm code/API in every snippet though libghostty is "đã chốt"; (3) an inconsistent TERM value (xterm-256color in doc 12 vs xterm-ghostty in docs 14/15) presented without reconciling which is authoritative; (4) completeness gaps for the current architecture — there is NO single authoritative architecture overview reflecting all final decisions (README points to doc 12 as "đọc đầu tiên" but doc 12 is a 103k re-scope essay, not a clean overview), NO consolidated decisions-log, and the glossary in README only covers the old GUI-video-path vocabulary and omits every current-architecture term (PTY, terminal path, GUI video path, libghostty, NetBird/WireGuard, P2P, TUI, A+B1, inspector, CoT, setup-token, OSC 133). Also found editorial artifacts left in doc 12 (LLM meta-commentary "Tôi đã viết xong section…" lines 251–263, a stray absolute file-path footer line 662, and a "Tôi đánh dấu…" first-person line at 14:55), and risk-ID disorder in doc 08 (R10 before R9; one risk has no R-number). Codename "PaneCast" is used consistently. Decisions are generally well-rationalized; the few that aren't are listed below.

issues.summary: The doc set's information architecture still reflects its origin as a game-streaming/screen-sharing project (docs 01-11), with the current terminal-first coding-hybrid architecture (docs 12-16) bolted on via per-doc "superseded/GUI-path-only" banners. The result misleads a new reader at the very first contact point: the README's title, scope table, and non-functional goals are written in the OLD game-streaming-then-coding framing ("text crispness is priority #1", "PaneCast — Low-Latency Per-Window Screen Sharing"), which directly contradicts doc 12's central decision to DROP text-crispness from the video path and lead with terminal text streaming. There is no single concise authoritative "read-first" overview that captures the current architecture + all final decisions: doc 12 is nominated as "read first" but is a 103k three-documents-in-one concatenation (two TL;DRs, two near-top-level sections, and leftover AI-generation meta-commentary at lines 251-263), too long and too internally seamed to serve that role. CURRENT vs HISTORICAL is signposted only by inconsistent, ad-hoc banners (5+ different phrasings/emoji conventions across 01/05/07/08/09/10/11), with no grouping in the README doc table and no decisions log. Several stale references remain (doc 01 and doc 13 still say terminal renders with SwiftTerm, superseded by libghostty in doc 12 §4; README terminology glossary contains only video-era terms). Recommended fix is light reorganization plus strong signposting: (1) add a short top-level ARCHITECTURE+DECISIONS overview (or rewrite the README top to BE it) that states the current terminal-first hybrid and links each final decision to its detailed doc; (2) standardize a single banner format and add a CURRENT vs REFERENCE grouping to the README doc table; (3) give an explicit reading order; (4) add a one-line-per-decision decisions log. Avoid mass renumbering.

issues.location: Title line 1 + Scope table row "Use-case" (line 16) + "Mục tiêu phi chức năng" line 22
issues.problem: The README — the first thing a reader sees — still frames the project in the old screen-sharing/text-crispness terms. Title: "PaneCast — Low-Latency Per-Window Screen Sharing & Remote Control". Line 22: "Độ nét text là ưu tiên #1 ... Apple HW encode chỉ 4:2:0 → text màu bị nhòe → cần chiến lược riêng (xem doc 12)". But the CURRENT architecture (doc 12 §1.2/§7, doc 09 banner) explicitly DROPS text-crispness/4:4:4 as a concern because text goes over the terminal PTY path (rendered crisp by libghostty, never through a codec), and 4:2:0 video is declared "đủ tốt". A new reader is told the central problem is exactly the thing the current design says is no longer a problem.
issues.recommended_fix: Rewrite the README top to lead with the terminal-first hybrid framing: title should reflect a remote-coding tool (terminal PTY + optional GUI mirror), not screen sharing. Replace "Độ nét text là ưu tiên #1 → cần chiến lược 4:2:0" with the current framing: text crispness is achieved by routing terminal/code through the PTY text-path (libghostty, no codec); 4:2:0 video is only for the demoted GUI-mirror path and is accepted as good-enough.

The top ToC (lines 22-25) only lists four anchors and does not match the actual heading tree; a reader cannot navigate 103k via that ToC. issues.recommended_fix: Split doc 12 into its natural parts: keep 12 as the concise hybrid overview (read-first), and move the terminal-streaming design, the GUI-video-path simplification, and the docs-changes/roadmap into separate numbered docs (e.g. 12a/12b or new 17/18, and fold the roadmap into doc 07 since it already supersedes 07). At minimum, fix the ToC to mirror every H2 and remove the duplicate TL;DR.

issues.location: Banner, line 3: "terminal đi PTY text-path (như SSH/mosh, render bằng SwiftTerm)"
issues.problem: Doc 01's re-scope banner states the terminal path renders with SwiftTerm. This is stale: doc 12 §4 (line 399) explicitly supersedes SwiftTerm with libghostty as the chosen client emulator (SwiftTerm demoted to fallback). A reader starting at doc 01 gets the wrong terminal-stack decision.
issues.recommended_fix: Change "render bằng SwiftTerm" to "render bằng libghostty (SwiftTerm = fallback)" in the doc 01 banner, or drop the library name and just link to doc 12 §4 for the emulator decision.

issues.location: §4 "Hệ quả: giữ thiết kế ĐƠN GIẢN", line 68: "Terminal = TCP byte-stream + SwiftTerm."
issues.problem: Doc 13 still names SwiftTerm as the terminal renderer, contradicting the libghostty decision finalized in doc 12 §4. Doc 13 is in the CURRENT cluster, so this stale reference is more confusing than the same slip in a historical doc.
issues.recommended_fix: Replace "+ SwiftTerm" with "+ libghostty" (or remove the renderer name — it is irrelevant to the transport point being made).

issues.location: Doc table (lines 29-46) and "Đọc theo vai trò" (lines 48-53)
issues.problem: The doc table lists 01-16 in chronological/numeric order with no visual separation of CURRENT vs HISTORICAL. A new reader sees 01-architecture, 02-host-capture-encode, ... 11-absolute-latency presented as the primary spine, then has to infer from inline stars and a footnote that the real current architecture starts at 12. The numbering order actively front-loads the obsolete game-streaming docs.
issues.recommended_fix: Group the doc table into sections with headers: "CURRENT architecture (read these)" = README/overview + 12 + 13 + 14 + 16 (+15 prior-art); "Reference — GUI video path (Phase 4, historical latency research)" = 01,02,03,04,05,09,10,11; "Superseded" = 07 (roadmap, replaced by 12). Add a one-line 'status' column (current / GUI-path-only / superseded).

issues.location: Banners/markers across the set: compare 01 line 3, 05 line 3, 07 line 3, 08 line 5, 09 line 3, 10 line 3, 11 line 3
issues.problem: The 'this doc is historical / GUI-path-only' status is conveyed by a different banner wording and emoji each time: 01 "Cập nhật kiến trúc (re-scope)", 05 "CHỈ áp dụng cho GUI video-path", 07 "Đã được ghi đè", 09 "Re-scope (hybrid)", 10 "Chỉ áp dụng cho GUI video-path", 11 "Floor analysis này CHỈ áp dụng cho GUI video-path". There is no standard, so a reader cannot tell at a glance whether a doc is fully superseded, partially valid for the GUI path, or just corrected.
issues.recommended_fix: Define a 3-state banner standard and apply it verbatim at the top of every non-current doc: STATUS: SUPERSEDED (see X) / STATUS: REFERENCE — GUI video path only (Phase 4) / STATUS: CURRENT. One sentence each, same emoji. Put the legend in the README.

issues.location: Whole set — no decisions log exists
issues.problem: Decisions were made iteratively across many research rounds and are buried in the doc that happened to record them (libghostty+own-patch in 12/14; TERM=xterm-ghostty in 14; OAuth+setup-token vs reuse credentials.json in 14/15; input box A+B1, B2 deferred in 14; read-only inspector + CoT=placeholder-only in 16; NetBird no-app-crypto in 13; terminal=Phase1/GUI=Phase4 in 12). A reader cannot get the list of final decisions without reading all of 12-16.
issues.recommended_fix: Add a DECISIONS.md (or a section in the overview): one line per decision with status (chốt/open) and a link to the detailed doc+section. e.g. 'Terminal emulator = libghostty + own external-backend patch (fallback SwiftTerm) — see 12 §4'. This doubles as the change-log the project currently lacks.

issues.location: §4 video config line 588 (queueDepth = 5) vs 02 line 49 (queueDepth = 3, default 8, 2 valid) vs 11 §1.1/C3 (default 8, use 2-3)
issues.problem: The CURRENT doc 12 recommends config.queueDepth = 5 ("giá trị Apple khuyến nghị") for the GUI video path, while doc 02 and doc 11 (which doc 12 §3.3 says to keep as the GUI-path API reference) say the real default is 8 and recommend 2-3 for low latency. Doc 12 §3.3 line 745 itself repeats "queueDepth default 8, 2 hợp lệ". So within the current cluster, doc 12 gives two different queueDepth numbers (5 in the code block, 2 in the keep-list) and contradicts the reference docs it points to.
issues.recommended_fix: Reconcile to one recommendation. For the idle-efficiency coding profile, decide whether 5 (higher, fewer drops) or 2-3 (lower latency) is intended and state why; ensure doc 12 §4 code block, doc 12 §3.3 keep-list, doc 02, and doc 11 agree or explicitly note the profile-specific override.

issues.location: §1 risk ordering: R10 (line 48) appears before R9 (line 52)
issues.problem: Risks are listed R1..R8, then R10, then R9 — out of numeric order. R8 was clearly inserted later (it breaks the original severity sort: green R5/R6 then yellow R7, then R8/R10/R9), and R9/R10 were appended in reverse. Minor, but in a risks register the ordering jump makes the reader double-check they didn't miss R9.
issues.recommended_fix: Reorder to R9 before R10 (or renumber the late additions). Optionally re-sort the whole list by severity as the header ("xếp theo mức độ") promises, since R7/R8/R9/R10 currently violate that sort.

issues.location: "Thuật ngữ" glossary (lines 55-66)
issues.problem: The glossary contains only video/game-streaming-era terms (Glass-to-glass latency, IDR/Keyframe, NALU, TCC, AX). None of the vocabulary a reader of the CURRENT docs needs is defined: PTY, NetBird/WireGuard mesh, libghostty, alt-screen/1049, kitty keyboard protocol, OSC 52/133/777, JSONL transcript / structured inspector, NDJSON, setup-token/OAuth. The glossary reinforces the impression the project is about video.
issues.recommended_fix: Add the terminal-first terms (PTY/forkpty, NetBird mesh, libghostty, alt-screen, kitty keyboard, OSC 52/133, JSONL transcript, NDJSON, setup-token) and either move the video-only terms (NALU, IDR, Glass-to-glass) into a 'GUI video path terms' sub-section or mark them as such.

issues.location: Whole doc; banner line 3 defers to "12 §Revised phased roadmap"
issues.problem: Doc 07 is wholly superseded by the revised roadmap in doc 12 §4 (lines 751-808), yet it remains a full standalone numbered doc with its own Phase 0-4 that use the OLD ordering (Phase 1 = MVP video, Phase 2 = input injection) which directly conflicts with the current ordering (Phase 1 = terminal, Phase 4 = GUI video). Two roadmaps with conflicting phase numbers exist in the set; a reader who lands on 07 first gets the obsolete plan.
issues.recommended_fix: Either (a) replace doc 07's body with the current roadmap (move the revised roadmap OUT of the giant doc 12 INTO doc 07, where a roadmap belongs) and keep the old phases only as an appendix labeled 'historical GUI-path plan'; or (b) reduce doc 07 to a stub that redirects to the canonical roadmap. Do not leave two full conflicting roadmaps.

issues.location: ToC anchors line 22-25 vs actual headings
issues.problem: The in-doc ToC lists 4 anchors (kiến-trúc-hybrid, terminal-text-streaming, gui-video-path, roadmap) but the document has many more H2/H3 sections (TL;DR, §1-§8, two TL;DRs, etc.) and a duplicate-titled section. The anchors also assume GitHub-style slugging of Vietnamese headings with diacritics, which is fragile. Navigation within the largest doc is effectively broken.
issues.recommended_fix: If doc 12 is split (recommended), this resolves itself. If kept whole, regenerate the ToC to list every section and verify each anchor resolves; consider plain-ASCII heading anchors.

issues.location: Codename line 3 "Codename tạm: PaneCast (đổi tự do)" and inconsistent usage across set
issues.problem: The product name "PaneCast" appears only in README (3x), 01 (6x), 04, 06, 07, 12 — i.e. only the old game-streaming-era docs use it; none of the current docs (13/14/15/16) name the product, and 14/15/16 refer to 'tool của ta'. The name itself ("PaneCast" = pane casting / screen casting) encodes the obsolete screen-sharing framing.
issues.recommended_fix: Decide a product name that fits the terminal-first identity (or keep PaneCast deliberately) and use it consistently, or drop the codename entirely until chosen. At minimum note in the overview that 'PaneCast' is the legacy codename from the screen-sharing era.

issues.location: Filename/number 15 sits between 14 (claude-code) and 16 (inspector) but is prior-art research, not architecture
issues.problem: Within the current cluster (12-16), the doc types are mixed: 12 = architecture, 13 = transport, 14 = integration design, 15 = prior-art research, 16 = feature design. 15 is reference material (competitor teardown) interleaved with design docs, with no signposting that it is background rather than a spec.
issues.recommended_fix: In the README grouping, label 15 as 'Reference — prior art' distinct from the design docs (12/13/14/16). No renumber needed; just classify it in the doc table.

structural_recommendations: Add a short top-level read-first overview (new 00-overview.md / ARCHITECTURE.md, or a rewritten README body) that: states the current terminal-first hybrid in a few sentences, embeds the consolidated decisions table, and points to deep-dive docs. This is the highest-leverage fix — it gives a new reader the correct mental model before they touch any historical doc.

Group the README doc table into three labeled tiers with a status column: CURRENT (overview + 12,13,14,16, plus 15 as prior-art reference), REFERENCE — GUI video path / Phase 4 (01,02,03,04,05,09,10,11), and SUPERSEDED (07). Stop presenting 01-11 as the primary spine.

Standardize a single banner format with three states (CURRENT / REFERENCE-GUI-path-only / SUPERSEDED-see-X) and apply it verbatim at the top of every doc; put the legend in the README. Replace the 5+ ad-hoc banner wordings currently in 01/05/07/08/09/10/11.

Publish a recommended reading order in the README: (1) overview/decisions, (2) 12 hybrid architecture, (3) 13 NetBird transport, (4) 14 Claude Code integration + input box, (5) 16 read-only inspector, (6) 15 prior-art for context; then 'only if working the GUI video path (Phase 4): 01,02,04,05,09,10,11'.

Add a one-line-per-decision DECISIONS log (or section) linking each finalized decision to the doc/section that details it. Use it as the project's change-log going forward so future re-scopes update one canonical list instead of re-bannering N docs.

Split the 103k doc 12 into its natural components: a concise hybrid-architecture overview (keep as 12 / promote to read-first), terminal-streaming design, GUI-video-path simplification, and move the revised roadmap into doc 07 (the roadmap's natural home). This removes the duplicate TL;DRs, the broken ToC, and the generation meta-commentary, and makes the read-first doc actually short.

Sweep stale library references so the whole current cluster agrees on libghostty as the chosen emulator (fix 01 banner and 13 §4 which still say SwiftTerm); and refresh the README glossary with terminal-first vocabulary (PTY, NetBird, libghostty, alt-screen, kitty keyboard, OSC 52/133, JSONL transcript, NDJSON, setup-token), demoting the video-only terms to a clearly-labeled GUI-path subsection.

Prefer this light reorganization (overview + grouping + banner standard + decisions log + splitting only the one oversized doc) over renumbering 01-16; renumbering would break the many inter-doc links (03/05/10/11 cross-reference each other and 12/13 reference them by number) for little reader benefit.
